kept.text:
$HOOD

ROBINHOOD Q1 2026 EARNINGS:

- Revenue $1.07B +15% YoY
- EPS $0.38 +3% YoY
- Adjusted EBITDA $534M +14% YoY
- Total Platform Assets $307B +39% YoY
- Robinhood Gold Subscribers 4.3M +36% YoY
- ARPU $157 +8% YoY

Overall, it was a strong quarter but fell short of expectations due to lower take rates across all assets. Trading volumes were strong and are improving in April, but the take rates lowered which led to a double miss.

I think the broader story so far in earnings has been around expectations vs reality and specifically if a company can decisively beat those expectations. The chip names, memory names, energy names, datacenter names are absolutely putting up beats of 50-100%, which was something Robinhood did last year.

Without those types of beats, which fintech and software haven't been able to give, it's just much harder for the street to rotate into those positions. 

$HOOD -9%

kept.text: $HYPE > $HOOD

profile_bio.description: President & Co-Founder of DASTAN: @MyriadMarkets + @RugRadio + @DecryptMedia | host @FOMOHOUR | press@rug.fm | opinions are my own

quoted_tweet.text:
Robinhood vs Hyperliquid Q1 results

Clear cyclical downtrend in crypto trading. Robinhood crypto revenue fell to $134 million (-39.4% QoQ), while Hyperliquid crypto trading revenue fell to $179.7 million (-31.0% QoQ).

Still, Hyperliquid’s crypto revenue was higher than Robinhood’s crypto line, while its RWA revenue grew +454.8% QoQ.

Users also grew 29.6% QoQ to 1.19M, versus Robinhood funded customers up roughly 1.5% QoQ.

HYPE’s discount to TradFi comps has largely been a cyclicality discount on crypto-linked cash flows. But with RWA now >30% of volumes, HIP-4 adding binary outcomes, and revenue volatility already roughly in line with Robinhood through the slowdown, that discount looks less justified.

At $192.3M of Q1 protocol income versus Robinhood’s $346 million of net income, HYPE looks incredibly cheap at a $9.5 billion circulating market cap against HOOD trading at $74 billion.

$HOOD is evolving from an options-led trading app into a diversified financial platform driven by crypto and new “other” revenue streams.

• Options remain the core engine today
• Crypto is becoming a major growth leg
• “Other” revenue like prediction markets, futures & new products is scaling fastest
• Equities are declining from ~35% of revenue in 2020 to ~12% by 2030
• Total revenue is expected to climb toward ~$4.2B by 2030

quoted_tweet.text:
$HOOD earnings after the close:

Robinhood ended 2025 with 4.18 million Gold subscribers, a 15.5% adoption rate compared to its 27 million funded customers.

As of February 2026, funded customers were 27.4 million.

Let’s say they added 600,000 more in March (quite aggressive), ending the quarter at 28 million funded customers.

To hit 4.6 million Gold subscribers, they need a 16.4% adoption rate (up from 15.5% in Q4 ‘25) based on 28 million customers.

I’m taking the NO on Robinhood hitting 4.6 million + subscribers in Q1 ‘26. We’ll see! 

NFA.

tweet.text:
$HOOD Reports Earnings Today: Here is my 5 year thesis + thoughts. 

In 2025, we saw $HOOD grow multiple legs and the market took notice. It wasn't all retail FOMO, it was a true turn around of the company from the "meme stock trading app." 

The most underrated part of this whole story is demographics. $HOOD is taking over generations of investors. Millennials and Gen Z market capture, in the early years of their earning years.

And it's not all people switching over for UI and incentives. New investors aren't leaving for Schwab. They're not going to Fidelity. They grew up and started on RH. Platform AUM is growing passively. 

This also comes with a swath of new products..RH gold subscription, credit cards, joint accounts, custodial accounts, strategies, prediction markets, international, etc. It is clear that Robinhood is setting up to be the financial super app.

Short term pain is crypto risks, as transactional revenue slumps as crypto seems almost boring. Although, I see a path towards crypto becoming a smaller % of the pie, just takes time. 

My 5 year outlook:

Base Case: The demographic flywheel compounds. Gold and subscriptions grow modestly. $HOOD  trades like a fintech compounder, not a speculation vehicle. Market Cap: ~$115B

Bull case: Full financial super-app. Brokerage, crypto, banking, prediction markets, retirement, international. AUM balloons as users age and earn more. Vlad's vision comes full circle. Market Cap: ~$250B

Average consensus PT from the street: ~$102

The 5 year story is real if you believe the demographic thesis and give them credit for actually building. The near-term could be more choppy. Trading -28% YTD.

Long $HOOD.

2. Robinhood Markets, Inc. ( $HOOD )
发布时间： 盘后 (Post-market)
市值： $76.3B
收入 Est： $1.14B
YoY 增长： 23.85%
看点： 关注其从交易平台向“金融超级 App”转型的进展，特别是 Gold 会员订阅增长和新推出的信用卡业务。

3. Teradyne, Inc. ( $TER )
发布时间： 盘后 (Post-market)
市值： $65.4B
收入 Est： $1.21B
YoY 增长： 79.19%
看点： 半导体测试业务受益于 AI 芯片（定制 ASIC 和 HBM）的复杂性提升，同时关注协作机器人业务的复苏。

4. Bloom Energy Corporation ( $BE )
发布时间： 盘后 (Post-market)
市值： $64.8B
收入 Est： $540.02M
YoY 增长： 84.09%
看点： 随着数据中心对稳定电力的紧迫需求，公司的燃料电池订单积压（Backlog）和大型公用事业合作是焦点。

5. Silicon Motion ( $SIMO )
发布时间： 盘后 (Post-market)
市值： $5.22B
收入 Est： $299.58M
YoY 增长： 84.12%
看点： 随着智能手机和 PC 进入 GenAI 硬件升级周期，公司在高性能控制器（PCIe Gen5）领域的份额扩张是主驱动力。
